description_long: This object is a large commercial jet airliner with a streamlined fuselage painted in teal and gray, featuring a prominent 'M' logo on the vertical stabilizer. The airplane has a low-wing configuration with swept-back wings and four cylindrical turbofan engines mounted under the wings, two on each side. The tail section is conventional, with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers extending from either side. The cockpit is located at the forward end of the fuselage, with a row of windows running along the length of the passenger cabin. The landing gear is not visible in the images, suggesting it may be retractable. The overall design is typical of long-range passenger aircraft used in commercial aviation.
description: A teal and gray commercial jet airliner with swept-back low wings, four underwing turbofan engines, and a conventional tail with a prominent logo.
description_view_0: Top-down view showing the full wingspan, fuselage, and tail configuration, highlighting the swept-back wings and engine placement.
description_view_1: Left side profile view displaying the fuselage windows, four underwing engines, and the 'M' logo on the tail.
description_view_2: Front view emphasizing the nose, cockpit windows, engine arrangement, and the symmetry of the wings.
description_view_3: Right side profile view similar to view 1, showing the opposite side of the fuselage, engines, and tail logo.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, swept-back wings with no visible winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Four underwing turbofan engines, two per wing.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Retractable tricycle landing gear (not visible but implied by design).
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multiple windows, typical of commercial airliners.
